title: cinnamomum neurophyllum
intro: Cinnamomum neurophyllum, also known as the Kanuka tree or the New Caledonian cinnamon, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Pacific island of New Caledonia. The bark of this plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of cinnamaldehyde and other volatile oils. The health benefits of Cinnamomum neurophyllum are said to include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark of Cinnamomum neurophyllum can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties and provide various health benefits.
